mechanism: PAL1 deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate, the entry step of phenylpropanoid metabolism
C4H (CYP73A) hydroxylates cinnamate at the 4-position to produce p-coumarate
4CL1 activates p-coumarate as its CoA thioester for downstream reactions
HCT transfers p-coumaroyl-CoA onto shikimate to yield p-coumaroyl-shikimate
CSE hydrolyses caffeoyl-shikimate into caffeic acid and shikimate
CCoAOMT methylates caffeoyl-CoA to feruloyl-CoA, the shared precursor of G and S branches
CCR reduces feruloyl-CoA to coniferaldehyde, the central G/S branch-point aldehyde
F5H1/CAld5H hydroxylates coniferaldehyde at the 5-position, committing flux to the S-branch
COMT methylates the 5-OH of 5-hydroxyconiferaldehyde to produce sinapaldehyde
CAD1 reduces coniferaldehyde to coniferyl alcohol, the G monolignol
CAD1 reduces sinapaldehyde to sinapyl alcohol, the S monolignol
Substrate flux via C4H: depletion of cinnamate (e.g., PAL1 KO) propagates to reduce p-coumarate pool
p-Coumarate is the 4CL substrate; depletion upstream reduces the CoA-thioester pool
CSE-mediated substrate flux; caffeoyl-shikimate is hydrolysed to caffeic acid
F5H1 substrate flux into the S-branch
F5H1 is the rate-limiting S-branch enzyme; its activity level is the dominant determinant of poplar S/G
Sinapyl alcohol is the S monolignol feeding the S numerator of the S/G ratio
PtrMYB152 OE in Arabidopsis raised S/G monomer ratio from 0.31 (WT) to 0.37-0.42
Tension wood fibres in WT poplar deposit a higher proportion of syringyl units than normal or opposite wood
Coniferyl alcohol is the G monolignol feeding the G denominator of the S/G ratio; excess G lowers the ratio
In poplar, C3H downregulation preferentially reduces G units at the expense of H accumulation, raising S/G ratio from ~1.7 to ~3.6 â€” encoded as C3H net-negative on S/G
Auxin represses SND1 orthologs during xylem cell expansion, delaying secondary-wall deposition until after expansion
PtrARF2.1 represses WND2B/WND6B (SND1 paralogs); RNAi lines show upregulated WND2B/6B
PdMYB221 feedback-represses master-switch SND1 for secondary-wall homeostasis
PtrSND1/WND2B binds SNBE sites in PtrMYB3 promoter
PtrWND activates PtrKNAT7 in the second-tier cascade
PtrWND3A/6B directly activates PtrCCoAOMT1 via SNBE
PtrSND1 (WND2B) activates PtrNAC156 in the second-tier cascade
PtrNAC156 expression is co-elevated with PtoMYB74 in transgenic OE lines, consistent with second-tier regulation
PtrMYB3 activates lignin biosynthetic genes including CCoAOMT1
MYB46-class MYBs activate MYB221/MYB4-class repressors as part of the homeostatic feedback
PtoMYB74 directly activates the CCR2 promoter in transactivation assays
PtrARF2.1 represses PtoMYB92; MYB92 is upregulated in PtrARF2.1-RNAi
PtrMYB090 binds and activates F5H promoter
PtrMYB161 binds and directly regulates PtrCAld5H1/2 (F5H1) promoters
PtNF-YA11 directly binds and activates PtF5H2 (F5H1) promoter
PtrWBLH2 is upstream regulator of F5H
PdMYB221 binds and represses PdCOMT2 promoter (EMSA)
PdMYB221 OE suppresses 9 lignin genes including CCoAOMT1

CCR2 activity level drives preferential S-unit deposition: downregulation in poplar produces an S/G ratio significantly lower than WT with S reduction larger than G reduction - a flux-level bias captured as a direct phenotype edge
4CL1 activity level drives preferential S-unit deposition in poplar: CRISPR-KO gives 34% S reduction vs 5% G reduction (pyMBMS); antisense lines show S/G decline from 1.8-2.3 to 1.1-1.4
PtoMYB74 directly activates CCOAOMT1 promoter via transactivation assay
